summary: Partially decompiled gm_1601 by matching gm_80162B98 and gm_801677C0, then reorganized related game-mode state layout so gm_1A45 accesses a nested struct inside gm_80479D58_t. The PR moved gm_80479D58_t out of gm_1A45.static.h into gm/types.h, added a new gm_801677C0_s struct and forward declaration, and updated prototypes to use concrete return and parameter types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS/u8*. No review discussion was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented gm_80162B98 as a void function that obtains a u32 pointer from gmMainLib_8015CD8C and writes back MAX(*temp_r3 + 1, -1).
Implemented gm_801677C0 to zero the first four byte fields and clear unk_20 and unk_28 of a struct gm_801677C0_s argument.
Changed gm_80162B98 declaration in gm_1601.h from UNK_RET gm_80162B98(UNK_PARAMS) to void gm_80162B98(void).
Changed gm_801677C0 declaration from void gm_801677C0(u8*) to void gm_801677C0(struct gm_801677C0_s* arg0).
Added a forward declaration for struct gm_801677C0_s in gm/forward.h.
Moved struct gm_80479D58_t definition from gm_1A45.static.h into gm/types.h and gave it a nested struct gm_801677C0_s member at offset 0x10.

Updated gm_1A45.c field references from flat gm_80479D58_t members such as unk_10, unk_12, unk_14, unk_20, unk_28, unk_30, unk_34, and unk_38_0 to nested accesses through gm_80479D58.unk_10 and temp_r25->unk_10.
smart_moves: Recognized that the tail of gm_80479D58_t is a reusable sub-structure rather than independent flat fields, allowing gm_801677C0 to operate on the same layout that gm_1A45 uses.
Used STATIC_ASSERTs for both the new sub-structure and containing structure, preserving layout constraints while changing the C representation.
Kept unknown names where semantics were not proven, while still improving structure and prototypes enough to unblock matching.
Moved shared game-mode type definitions into gm/types.h instead of leaving them local to gm_1A45.static.h, making the new gm_801677C0_s parameter type available to gm_1601.
decomp_lessons: When a function zeros a run of fields inside a larger global/state object, check whether that region should become a named nested struct reused by other modules.
Prototype cleanup can require type relocation: a function signature using a newly identified struct may need a forward declaration and a shared header definition.
Nested structs can improve matching by aligning source-level field access with the compiler's expected offsets without pretending to know final semantic names.
Preserve layout with STATIC_ASSERT when refactoring unknown structs, especially around bitfields and function-pointer arrays.
A partial match can still provide durable structural information for neighboring files, as shown by gm_1601 changes driving gm_1A45 state layout updates.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were retained: gm_80162B98, gm_801677C0, gm_801A45E8, gm_801A4D34.
New struct names followed existing address/global-based unknown-type style: gm_801677C0_s for the function parameter layout and gm_80479D58_t for the global state type.
Unknown fields kept generic x0/x1/x2/x3/x4 and unk_20/unk_28/unk_30/unk_34/unk_38_0 naming rather than assigning speculative semantic names.
Forward declarations use plain struct tags, e.g. struct gm_801677C0_s, when the full layout is supplied elsewhere.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched gm_80162B98 with a local pointer variable temp_r3 and a MAX expression on the incremented pointed-to value.
Matched gm_801677C0 by expressing chained zero assignments for adjacent fields: arg0->unk_20 = arg0->unk_28 = 0.
Used a nested struct at gm_80479D58_t offset 0x10 so byte fields x0/x1/x2/x3, function pointer array x4, u64 fields, callback, integer, and bitfields line up with prior flat offsets.
Converted accesses like gm_80479D58.unk_12 into gm_80479D58.unk_10.x2, indicating the former byte fields were part of the nested sub-structure.
Retained bitfield representation for unk_38_0 and unk_38_1 inside the new sub-structure.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_801677C0_s fields x0/x1/x2/x3/x4 or identify their semantics.
Search for additional callers of gm_801677C0 to confirm whether gm_801677C0_s is reused outside gm_80479D58_t.
Check later gm_1601 matching work for functions adjacent to gm_80162B98 and gm_801677C0.
Investigate whether gm_80479D58_t.unk_10 represents screenshot/debug/frame capture state based on gm_1A45 usage around lb_80019A30 and db_CheckScreenshot.
